title: József Attila Móring

infobox.value: (born 8 October 1968)[[1]](#cite_note-Biography-1) is a Hungarian educator and politician, [member of the National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_of_Hungary "National Assembly of Hungary") (MP) for [Balatonboglár](/wiki/Balatonbogl%C3%A1r "Balatonboglár") ([Somogy County](/wiki/Somogy_County "Somogy County") Constituency IV) from 2006 to 2014, and for [Marcali](/wiki/Marcali "Marcali") ([Somogy County 3rd constituency](/wiki/Somogy_County_3rd_constituency "Somogy County 3rd constituency")) since 2014. He was also Member of Parliament from his party, the [Fidesz](/wiki/Fidesz "Fidesz")'s Somogy County Regional List between 2002 and 2004.[[2]](#cite_note-Register-2)

["Navracsics bemutatta öt területi kormánybiztosát, akik az uniós pénzek behajtásáért felelnek"](https://telex.hu/belfold/2022/05/30/navracsics-bemutatta-ot-teruleti-kormanybiztosat-akik-az-unios-penzek-behajtasaert-felelnek). *telex* (in Hungarian). 2022-05-30. Retrieved 2022-05-30.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Hungarian educator and politician

The native form of this [personal name](/wiki/Personal_name "Personal name") is *Móring József Attila*. This article uses [Western name order](/wiki/Personal_name#Western_name_order "Personal name") when mentioning individuals.

**József Attila Móring** (born 8 October 1968)[[1]](#cite_note-Biography-1) is a Hungarian educator and politician, [member of the National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_of_Hungary "National Assembly of Hungary") (MP) for [Balatonboglár](/wiki/Balatonbogl%C3%A1r "Balatonboglár") ([Somogy County](/wiki/Somogy_County "Somogy County") Constituency IV) from 2006 to 2014, and for [Marcali](/wiki/Marcali "Marcali") ([Somogy County 3rd constituency](/wiki/Somogy_County_3rd_constituency "Somogy County 3rd constituency")) since 2014. He was also Member of Parliament from his party, the [Fidesz](/wiki/Fidesz "Fidesz")'s Somogy County Regional List between 2002 and 2004.[[2]](#cite_note-Register-2)

## Political career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=J%C3%B3zsef_Attila_M%C3%B3ring&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Political career")]

He served as mayor of [Somogyvár](/wiki/Somogyv%C3%A1r "Somogyvár") from 1998 to 2014. He joined Fidesz in December 1992. He led the Somogyvár branch of his party until 2002. He became a Member of Parliament in December 2002, replacing [Károly Szita](/w/index.php?title=K%C3%A1roly_Szita&action=edit&redlink=1 "Károly Szita (page does not exist)"), who resigned from his parliamentary seat.[[1]](#cite_note-Biography-1) Móring worked in the Committee on Human Rights, Minorities and Religious Affairs between 2003 and 2006. He was elected MP for Balatonboglár during the [2006 parliamentary election](/wiki/2006_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2006 Hungarian parliamentary election") as a candidate of the Fidesz–[KDNP](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_People%27s_Party_(Hungary) "Christian Democratic People's Party (Hungary)") alliance. He joined the KDNP parliamentary group. He was a member of the Immunity, Incompatibility and Credentials Committee between 2006 and 2010. He was elected one of the recorders of the National Assembly in May 2006.[[2]](#cite_note-Register-2)

Móring was re-elected MP for Balatonboglár in the [2010 parliamentary election](/wiki/2010_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2010 Hungarian parliamentary election"). He became a member of the National Security Committee in May 2010. He was Vice Chairman of that ad hoc committee which intended to investigate the [Civil Guard Association for a Better Future](/wiki/Civil_Guard_Association_for_a_Better_Future "Civil Guard Association for a Better Future")'s activity in [Gyöngyöspata](/wiki/Gy%C3%B6ngy%C3%B6spata "Gyöngyöspata") in early 2011. After the [2014 parliamentary election](/wiki/2014_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2014 Hungarian parliamentary election"), which adopted a new election law and redrawn constituencies, Móring was elected MP for Marcali ([Somogy County 3rd constituency](/wiki/Somogy_County_3rd_constituency "Somogy County 3rd constituency")).[[2]](#cite_note-Register-2) He was re-elected in 2018 and 2022. Following the [2022 parliamentary election](/wiki/2022_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2022 Hungarian parliamentary election"), Minister [Tibor Navracsics](/wiki/Tibor_Navracsics "Tibor Navracsics") appointed him government commissioner responsible for the economic development of [Southern Transdanubia](/wiki/Southern_Transdanubia "Southern Transdanubia") in May 2022.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
